codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation of LuxAlgo Order Block detector as (order_block, luxalgo_v1) variant. Correctly implements all 5 key differentiators from a8ra_v1: extreme-candle anchor selection in structure interval, ATR filter, wick-to-wick zones, mitigation on touch, invalidation on close through. Properly consumes upstream LuxAlgo MSS results with upstream_refs linking. Registered in cascade build_default_registry(). 29 tests covering all VAL-LOB assertions. Two non-blocking issues found related to lifecycle scan start position and test assertion strength.

issues.description: Lifecycle scan starts from anchor_idx+1 instead of mss_bar_index+1. In LuxAlgo's PineScript, mitigation/invalidation tracking begins AFTER the break event (MSS bar). Bars between anchor and MSS bar are within the structure interval and could prematurely trigger mitigation or invalidation. Should scan from mss_bar_index+1 to match reference behavior. Practical impact is likely minimal (extreme anchor tends to have prices moving away), but edge cases exist where bars in the structure interval could trigger early state changes.

test_atr_filter_can_reject asserts len(ob_result.detections) <= len(mss_result.detections), which is trivially true by construction (every OB comes from an MSS). The test doesn't verify the ATR filter actually rejected any candles. A stronger test would run with two different atr_filter_multiplier values and assert strict inequality between the resulting OB counts, or verify that a known-volatile candle was excluded.

test_anchor_is_extreme_candle only verifies anchor_bar_index is present and is an int, but does not verify the selected candle is actually the most extreme in the structure interval. A stronger test would independently compute the extreme candle and compare against the detector's selection.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Lifecycle scan in _compute_lifecycle starts from anchor_idx+1 instead of mss_bar_index+1, potentially causing premature mitigation/invalidation for bars within the structure interval before the MSS break occurred. Reference LuxAlgo PineScript tracks lifecycle from the break bar onward.
test_atr_filter_can_reject has a trivially-true assertion (OB count <= MSS count is always true by design). Does not verify the filter actually rejects candles.
test_anchor_is_extreme_candle does not verify the anchor is actually the most extreme candle in the interval — only checks the field exists and is an int.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ra-engine-worker skill's TDD procedure (Steps 3-4) focuses on baseline fixture regression tests with exact count matching. For new variant detectors (LuxAlgo MSS/OB), no baseline fixtures exist. The worker adapted by writing behavioral and property-based tests instead. The skill should document this alternative TDD approach for variant features without baseline fixtures, as noted in the worker's own skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md Step 3 says 'Read the relevant baseline fixture(s)' and Step 4 shows exact count assertions like 'assert len(result.detections) == 345'. Worker's skillFeedback states: 'For variant detectors without baseline fixtures, the skill could mention that TDD still applies but regression tests are replaced with behavioral/property-based tests since there's no baseline to match against.'

summary: PASS. LuxAlgoOBDetector is a solid clean-room implementation correctly registered as (order_block, luxalgo_v1). All 5 VAL-LOB assertions are covered: zone creation from upstream MSS (VAL-LOB-001), extreme-candle anchor with ATR filter (VAL-LOB-002), wick-to-wick zones (VAL-LOB-003), mitigation/invalidation lifecycle (VAL-LOB-004), and valid Detection schema with upstream_refs (VAL-LOB-005). 29 tests pass, 704 total with zero regression. Three non-blocking issues found: lifecycle scan starting too early (from anchor instead of MSS bar), and two tests with weak assertions that don't fully verify the behaviors they claim to test. No blocking issues. Worker followed skill procedure and AGENTS.md conventions correctly.
